structural_claim: Quantum mechanics fundamentally alters the behavior of matter at extreme densities, creating new states that resist gravitational collapse through quantum degeneracy pressure

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Quantum degeneracy pressure from the Pauli exclusion principle creates a fundamental mass threshold that determines stellar endpoints
Fermionic wavefunctions must be antisymmetric, fundamentally constraining how particles can occupy quantum states

supporting_results.result_name: Neutron Star Equation of State Constraints
supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Nuclear forces must stiffen dramatically at high density to support massive neutron stars against gravitational collapse
